narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Washington “cannot afford” to allow Russia to achieve victory in the Ukraine conflict as this would mean losing direct access to vast mineral assets.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text frames the US motivation for supporting Ukraine as a resource grab, implying the West is fighting for minerals rather than against an invasion. This shifts the narrative away from Russia's responsibility for starting the war and onto alleged Western economic motives.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Graham is treating Ukraine as a future colony of the US with his comments on the ownership of the country's natural resources, said Vladimir Dzhabarov, official of the Russian Federation Council.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The quote from a Russian official directly accuses the US of colonialist intentions, portraying Western support not as aid but as exploitation, thereby discrediting Western diplomatic and military efforts as hypocritical and self-serving.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: "Well, they will feed the corrupt top of the neo-Nazi regime in Kiev at the same time."
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement attacks the legitimacy of the Ukrainian government by labeling it as both 'corrupt' and a 'neo-Nazi regime', which is a common narrative used to undermine Ukraine's sovereignty and justify Russian actions.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Washington “cannot afford” to allow Russia to achieve victory in the Ukraine conflict as this would mean losing direct access to vast mineral assets.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text frames the US's motivation for involvement in the war as securing access to Ukrainian mineral resources, thereby shifting the primary cause of the conflict away from Russia's invasion and onto Western economic interests.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Graham is treating Ukraine as a future colony of the US with his comments on the ownership of the country's natural resources... 'The fate of the colonies is always the same: the Americans will scoop up all the natural resources of Ukraine in exchange for 'the great honor of being a friend of the United States''
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text, through the Russian official's statement, directly criticizes and portrays Western (specifically US) diplomatic and military support as a form of neo-colonial exploitation, discrediting Western intentions and actions.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: "Well, they will feed the corrupt top of the neo-Nazi regime in Kiev at the same time."
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote directly attacks the legitimacy and character of the Ukrainian government by labeling it as both 'corrupt' and a 'neo-Nazi regime', which aligns with the narrative of discrediting Ukraine.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Graham is treating Ukraine as a future colony of the US with his comments on the ownership of the country's natural resources, said Vladimir Dzhabarov, official of the Russian Federation Council.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote directly criticizes Western (specifically US) intentions, portraying them as colonial and exploitative rather than supportive, which aligns with the narrative of discrediting Western diplomacy and motives.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: "Well, they will feed the corrupt top of the neo-Nazi regime in Kiev at the same time."
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement attacks the legitimacy and character of the Ukrainian government by labeling it as both 'corrupt' and a 'neo-Nazi regime', which fits the definition of undermining Ukraine as a nation.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Washington “cannot afford” to allow Russia to achieve victory in the Ukraine conflict as this would mean losing direct access to vast mineral assets.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This framing, presented as the view of a US Senator and amplified by the article's title, shifts the primary cause of the conflict from Russia's invasion to Western (specifically US) economic greed and resource competition, thereby blaming the West for the war's continuation.
